title: Media-sourcing pipeline (open image/audio license verification + attribution)
context: As deck volume grows, manually verifying licenses for each image and audio file becomes a bottleneck. An automated media-sourcing pipeline that searches approved open media repositories, verifies license terms, downloads files, and generates attribution records scales the media path beyond hand-curation. This reduces reviewer burden and ensures media license compliance at the same level as text sources.
objective: Build a media-sourcing pipeline that searches approved open image/audio repositories, verifies license terms, downloads files with provenance records, and integrates with the license-verification gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Searches at least one approved open media repository (e.g., Wikimedia Commons) for images and/or audio matching a search term.
Verifies license terms for each candidate file; excludes files with non-open licenses per the research-002 rules.
Downloads approved files and records per-file SPDX license identifier, source URL, attribution string, and retrieval date in a provenance sidecar.
Integrates with the code-003 license-verification gate: downloaded media provenance passes the same 'unknown = excluded' check.
Generates attribution strings ready to embed in CrowdAnki JSON card fields.
Licensed MIT; includes unit tests and a README.
output: A merged PR adding a media-sourcing pipeline CLI (tools/source-media/) with Wikimedia Commons integration, provenance recording, and license gate integration; licensed MIT.
